architecture.summary: SketchGCD is a two-phase constrained decoding system for blackbox LLMs without logit access. In phase 1 (sketching), a powerful blackbox LLM generates an unconstrained preliminary output (sketch) given an instruction and few-shot demonstrations. In phase 2 (constrained decoding), a locally hosted smaller auxiliary LLM refines the sketch to satisfy specified constraints by applying grammar-constrained decoding. Information flows from the blackbox LLM's text output → auxiliary LLM's constrained refinement → final output.

biblio.title: Sketch-Guided Constrained Decoding for Boosting Blackbox Large Language Models without Logit Access

claims.statement: On Wiki-NRE closed information extraction, SketchGCD with GPT-4 sketcher and LLaMA-2-7B decoder achieves F1 of 46.1, compared to 43.6 for GPT-4 unconstrained baseline
On SynthIE-text closed information extraction, SketchGCD with GPT-3.5-Turbo sketcher and LLaMA-2-7B decoder achieves F1 of 45.1, compared to 23.8 for GPT-3.5-Turbo unconstrained baseline
On Penn Treebank constituency parsing with GPT-4 sketcher, SketchGCD achieves 100% tag validity (6.4 points improvement), compared to 93.6% for GPT-4 unconstrained
On Penn Treebank constituency parsing with GPT-4 sketcher, SketchGCD achieves 92.5% tree validity, compared to 86.0% for GPT-4 unconstrained
Beam search is critical for constrained decoding performance: on Wiki-NRE with LLaMA-2-7B, beam size 1 (greedy) yields F1 of 25.8, beam size 2 yields 32.8, and beam size 8 yields 33.4

SketchGCD achieves 100% grounding precision by construction: all entities and relations in output are guaranteed to be present in the knowledge catalogue, compared to 45.2% invalid triplets for GPT-4 unconstrained on Wiki-NRE
SketchGCD adds computational overhead as it requires a constrained decoder to refine sketches after the sketching phase, incurring additional latency and resource costs
SketchGCD can only enforce constraints at the structure or syntactic level, not at the semantic level; the model can still generate semantically incorrect outputs
